title: Immunofluorescence - RAW 264.7
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fne7v","text":"This protocol details steps taken to perform immunofluorescence in RAW 264.7 cells.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Plate 2 x 10^5 RAW 264.7 cells on poly-D-lysine coated coverslips.
Add the indicated compound at the provided concentration and time point in fresh DMEM media containing FBS and P/S.
Proceed to next step if not treating cells.
Fix cells in 4% paraformaldehyde supplemented with 4% sucrose for 00:30:00 at Room temperature .
Wash coverslips 3X for 00:05:00 with PBS at Room temperature .
Add primary antibodies Overnight at 4 °C in 3% BSA in PBS.
Wash coverslips 3X for 00:05:00 with PBS at room temperature.
Add secondary antibody for 01:00:00 at Room temperature in PBS in the dark.
Wash coverslips 3X for 00:05:00 with PBS at Room temperature .
Mount coverslips onto microscope slides with Prolong Gold mounting media and store at 4 °C .
creator: Amanda Bentley-DeSousa
